what_it_does: Proposes a patch to runtime/bridge.py that adds a new POST endpoint /api/territory/write to the allowlist and implements a handler calling runtime.territory.territory_write to persist gallery direction marks at code:// element sub-addresses.
observations: Patch targets bridge.py allowlist near /api/annotate and a do_POST handler near line 2517
Handler accepts element_id and an items array (or a single item fallback) and returns ok, written count, and marks
Imports runtime.territory.territory_write inside the handler body
Fail-loud behavior on bad items is expected to surface as a 400 through the existing do_POST try/except
Claims the route is additive and changes no existing behavior
Pairs with runtime.territory.territory_write and the FE hook 2 in fork-gallery-brain-hooks.js
Includes verify-after-apply steps for a comment mark round-trip and a bad-item 400
Authored as a fabric-derived fork-authored proposed diff with trust/author/date metadata
standouts: Route-back design: gallery:direction binder → hook 2 groups by element_id → POST /api/territory/write → suite.mark → gallery:rerender
Explicitly distinguishes this path from the ui://-gated /api/annotate and from /api/submit_response
Fail-loud error handling instead of silently swallowing bad items
Single-file patch presented as file-disjoint, meet-at-the-address integration
conventions: Follows additive route registration placed beside existing /api/annotate
Uses existing _body/_send helper pattern in the handler
Uses a structured proposed-diff header with trust, author, date, and pairs-with metadata
concerns: Imports territory_write inside the request handler rather than at module top
Handler builds the items list from b.get('item') without visible per-item shape validation
No explicit authz check is shown beyond the existing do_POST try/except
Safety claims rely on territory_write fail-loud behavior that is not visible in this file
notes: A proposed bridge patch that wires a new gallery-direction write endpoint through the existing territory_write mark store.
questions: What is the exact input validation and failure shape of runtime.territory.territory_write?
How does the existing do_POST try/except format and return fail-loud errors?
What is the runtime cost of importing runtime.territory inside a hot request handler?
How does the frontend hook handle partial failures when multiple items are posted?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to add a POST write backend for gallery direction marks; the file does exactly that by proposing allowlist and handler changes plus verification steps.
apparent_themes: File-disjoint collaboration between fork and projection
Route-back persistence for UI gallery directions
intention_signals: It is trying to give the frontend gallery a safe, dedicated write path that lands direction marks at code:// element sub-addresses without going through the ui://-gated annotate endpoint, then re-renders from the mark store.
novelty: Distinct in explicitly framing a route-back endpoint as a bridge-owner file patch applied by projection while the fork only authors the disjoint design file, and in justifying safety by contrasting with ui://-gated annotate and /api/submit_response.
contribution: Contributes a concrete, minimal, additive bridge patch design with a handler shape, allowlist change, safety rationale, and verification steps that a fused system could keep as the specification for wiring /api/territory/write.
summary_for_embedding: Proposed patch document for adding a POST /api/territory/write endpoint to runtime/bridge.py that delegates to runtime.territory.territory_write to persist gallery direction marks at code:// element sub-addresses.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a fail-loud, scheme-agnostic write backend for frontend gallery direction marks that avoids the ui://-gated annotate path and triggers re-render from the mark store.
